fact: April 24 is exactly 114 days into the year - that's the same number as how many bones a snake has in its skull!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for cherry blossoms to carpet the ground like pink snow after windy days.
In Alaska, the sun now stays up for almost 19 hours straight by this date.
Taurus season is in full swing - people born today are stubborn as bulls but also love cozy blankets and snacks.
In Australia and New Zealand, autumn leaves are typically crunching underfoot and temperatures are dropping fast.
This date falls in the middle of peak allergy season - tree pollen is exploding everywhere like nature's glitter bomb!
Migrating hummingbirds are usually zooming through North America right now, beating their wings 80 times per second.
The moon phase around April 24 changes every year, but when it's a new moon, you can see way more stars than usual.
In northern countries, this is often when ice finally melts on lakes, making loud cracking sounds like giant Rice Krispies.
April 24 is exactly 2 months and 3 days after the spring equinox - nature is officially in full wake-up mode.
Dandelions are typically going crazy right now, turning into those fluffy wish-balls that kids blow everywhere.
In places like Japan, this is when wisteria vines often explode with purple flowers that hang down like grape clusters.
